source_paper_title: Proximity and Flatness Bounds for Linear Integer Optimization
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Celaya_2024, title={Proximity and Flatness Bounds for Linear Integer Optimization}, volume={49}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2022.0335}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2022.0335}, number={4}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Celaya, Marcel and Kuhlmann, Stefan and Paat, Joseph and Weismantel, Robert}, year={2024}, month=nov, pages={2446–2467} }
context:
Page 3, Introduction (discussion following Theorem 4)

It is open whether the lattice width of lattice-free polytopes can be bounded solely by \(\Delta_n(A)\).
title: Bound lattice width of lattice-free polytopes using only determinant minor parameter
background_latex:
Let $A\in\mathbb{Z}^{m\times n}$ have full column rank and let $b\in\mathbb{Z}^m$. Define the (rational) polyhedron
\[
P(A,b):=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: Ax\le b\}.
\]
The polyhedron is called lattice-free if $P(A,b)\cap\mathbb{Z}^n=\emptyset$.

For a nonzero direction $a\in\mathbb{R}^n\setminus\{0\}$, define the width of $P(A,b)$ in direction $a$ by
\[
w_a(P(A,b)):=\max_{x\in P(A,b)} a^\top x-\min_{y\in P(A,b)} a^\top y.
\]
The lattice width is
\[
w(P(A,b)):=\min_{a\in\mathbb{Z}^n\setminus\{0\}} w_a(P(A,b)).
\]

For $k\in\{1,\dots,n\}$ define the maximal absolute $k\times k$ minor of $A$ by
\[
\Delta_k(A):=\max\{ |\det M| : M \text{ is a } k\times k \text{ submatrix of } A\}.
\]
In particular, $\Delta_n(A)$ is the maximum absolute determinant of an $n\times n$ submatrix of $A$ (which is nonzero because $A$ has full column rank $n$).
problem_definition_latex:
Determine whether there exists a function $f:\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1}\to\mathbb{R}_{\ge 0}$ such that for every dimension $n\ge 2$, every full-column-rank matrix $A\in\mathbb{Z}^{m\times n}$, and every $b\in\mathbb{Z}^m$ for which $P(A,b)$ is a (full-dimensional) lattice-free polyhedron, the lattice width satisfies
\[
w(P(A,b))\le f(\Delta_n(A)).
\]
Equivalently, decide whether the lattice width of lattice-free polytopes can be bounded above by a function depending only on $\Delta_n(A)$ (and not on $n$).
latex:
Let $A\in\mathbb{Z}^{m\times n}$ have full column rank and let $b\in\mathbb{Z}^m$. Define the (rational) polyhedron
\[
P(A,b):=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: Ax\le b\}.
\]
The polyhedron is called lattice-free if $P(A,b)\cap\mathbb{Z}^n=\emptyset$.

For a nonzero direction $a\in\mathbb{R}^n\setminus\{0\}$, define the width of $P(A,b)$ in direction $a$ by
\[
w_a(P(A,b)):=\max_{x\in P(A,b)} a^\top x-\min_{y\in P(A,b)} a^\top y.
\]
The lattice width is
\[
w(P(A,b)):=\min_{a\in\mathbb{Z}^n\setminus\{0\}} w_a(P(A,b)).
\]

For $k\in\{1,\dots,n\}$ define the maximal absolute $k\times k$ minor of $A$ by
\[
\Delta_k(A):=\max\{ |\det M| : M \text{ is a } k\times k \text{ submatrix of } A\}.
\]
In particular, $\Delta_n(A)$ is the maximum absolute determinant of an $n\times n$ submatrix of $A$ (which is nonzero because $A$ has full column rank $n$).

Determine whether there exists a function $f:\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1}\to\mathbb{R}_{\ge 0}$ such that for every dimension $n\ge 2$, every full-column-rank matrix $A\in\mathbb{Z}^{m\times n}$, and every $b\in\mathbb{Z}^m$ for which $P(A,b)$ is a (full-dimensional) lattice-free polyhedron, the lattice width satisfies
\[
w(P(A,b))\le f(\Delta_n(A)).
\]
Equivalently, decide whether the lattice width of lattice-free polytopes can be bounded above by a function depending only on $\Delta_n(A)$ (and not on $n$).
open_problem_quote: It is open whether the lattice width of lattice-free polytopes can be bounded solely by \(\Delta_n(A)\).
counterexample_suitability_rationale: One can computationally search for families of lattice-free polytopes with fixed $\Delta_n(A)$ and large lattice width by enumerating small integer matrices and right-hand sides and checking lattice-freeness and width via MILP/LP. However, the search space grows quickly with $n,m$ and certifying worst-case growth may require guided constructions beyond brute force.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate counterexamples can be represented programmatically as integer matrices $A$ and integer vectors $b$, with fitness given by computed lattice width subject to a constraint on $\Delta_n(A)$ and lattice-freeness. The challenge is efficiently enforcing full-dimensional lattice-freeness and exploring high-dimensional instances, but evolutionary search could plausibly discover structured families with large width or suggest sharper conjectured bounds.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The source paper (Celaya–Kuhlmann–Paat–Weismantel) frames the question of whether the lattice width \(w(P(A,b))\) of a full-dimensional lattice-free polyhedron \(P(A,b)=\{x:Ax\le b\}\) with integral right-hand side can be bounded by a function of \(\Delta_n(A)\) alone, independent of the dimension \(n\). Their main flatness advance is Theorem 4, which shows the existence of a facet normal (a row \(a\) of \(A\)) with facet width \(w_a(P(A,b)) < \frac{4n+2}{9}\,\Delta_n(A)-1\) under mild nonredundancy assumptions. This is obtained by linking flatness to a proximity parameter \(\pi(A)\) and proving refined proximity bounds (Theorem 5) in terms of \(\Delta_\alpha(A)\), using Minkowski’s theorem and a planar polygon area lower bound for sets satisfying \(\tau Q\subseteq Q^\circ\).

Despite these improvements, the bound still scales linearly with \(n\), and the determinant-only (dimension-free) bound remains open in general. The paper identifies regimes where dimension-free behavior does hold: for matrices whose \(n\times n\) minors lie in \(\{0,\pm k,\pm 2k\}\) one gets facet-width \(\le \Delta_n(A)-2\) (Theorem 6), extending earlier results for strictly \(\Delta\)-modular matrices. It also points to classes such as simplices and special pyramids (via prior work) where width can be controlled by \(\Delta_n(A)\).

The forward-citing works provided here are adjacent rather than resolving the width question. They develop determinant-parameterized structure and algorithms for bounded-subdeterminant matrices (e.g., threshold phenomena for \(\ell_\infty\)-SVP and strongly polynomial algorithms for structured totally \(\Delta\)-modular IPs), which may offer tools to control geometric complexity (faces, decompositions) in terms of \(\Delta\). However, none establishes a bound of the form \(w(P(A,b))\le f(\Delta_n(A))\) independent of \(n\), so the problem remains open.

sources.title: Totally -Modular IPs with Two Non-zeros in Most Rows
sources.theorem_or_result: The paper proves algorithmic tractability for a structured class of totally \(\Delta\)-modular integer programs: if \(M=\begin{bmatrix}A & B\\ W & D\end{bmatrix}\in\mathbb Z^{m\times n}\) is totally \(\Delta\)-modular, \(A\) has at most two nonzeros per row, and the block sizes \(m_2,n_2\le k\) are constant, then Problem 2 (and via reduction Problem 3) can be solved in strongly polynomial time (Theorem 4). A key structural ingredient is that for \(A\in\{0,\pm1\}^{m\times n}\) with two nonzeros per row viewed as a signed-graph incidence matrix, and a weight vector \(w\), the maximum alternating weight of a tree \(\mathrm{alt}(S,w)\) satisfies \(\mathrm{alt}(S,w)\le \Delta\big(\begin{bmatrix}A\\ w^\top\end{bmatrix}\big)\) (Proposition 11), enabling a bounded-adhesion tree-decomposition when subdeterminants are bounded (Theorem 16).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for a dimension-free upper bound on the lattice width \(w(P(A,b))\) of lattice-free polyhedra \(P(A,b)=\{x:Ax\le b\}\) in terms of \(\Delta_n(A)\) only. This paper does not study lattice-free polyhedra or lattice width/flatness bounds directly, nor does it provide a bound of the form \(w(P(A,b))\le f(\Delta_n(A))\). Instead it is algorithmic work on integer programs with bounded subdeterminants, using proximity bounds (Cook–Gerards–Schrijver–Tardos and improvements) and new graph-structural decompositions for matrices with (mostly) two nonzeros per row. These techniques are adjacent to the broader theme of proximity/flatness for bounded-minor matrices, but they do not resolve (or explicitly advance) the specific lattice-width question posed.

sources.title: On matrices over a polynomial ring with restricted subdeterminants
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper develops structural results for integral/polynomial matrices with restricted subdeterminants. In particular:
- Lemma 2: for finite S \(\subseteq \mathbb Z[x]\), the set \(F(S)\) of determinants of forbidden minors is finite and \(\max_{f\in F(S)}|f|\le 2\,(\max_{s\in S}|s|)^2\).
- Theorem 1 (rank-1 parametric decomposition): if \(M=T+xuv^\top\in\mathbb Z[x]^{m\times n}\) is totally \(S\)-modular, then \(T\) is totally \(S(0)\)-modular.
- Theorems 2–3: for specific finite sets \(S(a)=\pm\{0,1,a,a+1,2a+1\}\) or \(\pm\{0,a,a+1,2a+1\}\), recognition of totally \(S(a)\)-modular matrices and ILP with such constraint matrices are solvable in polynomial time (for all but finitely many exceptional \(a\)).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for a dimension-free bound on lattice width \(w(P(A,b))\) of lattice-free polyhedra in terms of \(\Delta_n(A)\) only. This paper studies matrices with *all* subdeterminants restricted to a finite set \(S\) (or polynomial analogues), yielding finiteness/structure and algorithmic results for such matrix classes. It does not address lattice-free polyhedra widths, flatness constants, or provide bounds of \(w(P(A,b))\) in terms of \(\Delta_n(A)\). At most, it is tangentially related because both lines of work use determinant/minor parameters (\(\Delta\)) to control integer-programming behavior.

sources.title: A Threshold Phenomenon for the Shortest Lattice Vector Problem in the Infinity Norm
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper proves a determinant-parameterized threshold phenomenon for the shortest lattice vector problem in the infinity norm for lattices of the form \(A\mathbb Z^n\) with \(A\in\mathbb Z^{m\times n}\) full column rank and \(\Delta:=\Delta_n(A)\). Defining \(f(\Delta):=\max\{n: \|Az\|_\infty\ge 2\ \forall z\in\mathbb Z^n\setminus\{0\}\ \text{for some \(\Delta\)-modular }A\}\), they show \(f(\Delta)\) is finite and satisfy
- \(\Delta-1\le f(\Delta)\le \lfloor(\Delta-1)/2\rfloor(\Delta-1)+\mathbf 1_{2\mathbb Z}(\Delta)\).
Consequently, if \(n\ge \lfloor(\Delta-1)/2\rfloor(\Delta-1)+\mathbf 1_{2\mathbb Z}(\Delta)+1\), then either one can find \(z^*\in\mathbb Z^n\setminus\{0\}\) with \(\|Az^*\|_\infty=1\) or certify \(\Delta_n(A)>\Delta\); this yields an FPT algorithm for \(\min_{z\ne 0}\|Az\|_\infty\) parameterized by \(\Delta\).
sources.connection: The open problem asks whether the lattice width \(w(P(A,b))\) of a full-dimensional lattice-free polyhedron \(P(A,b)=\{x:Ax\le b\}\) can be bounded by a function of \(\Delta_n(A)\) alone, independent of dimension \(n\). This paper does not address lattice-free polyhedra or lattice width directly, and it does not prove any bound of the form \(w(P(A,b))\le f(\Delta_n(A))\). However, it develops determinant-only structural results for \(\Delta\)-modular matrices and derives polyhedral consequences (e.g., integer-hull vertices lie on faces of dimension \(\le F(\Delta)\)). Such threshold/face-dimension phenomena are conceptually related to flatness/width questions and may provide tools toward bounding geometric complexity of integer-feasible regions in terms of \(\Delta\), but no explicit width bound for lattice-free sets is obtained here.
